title: Horkelia clevelandii
intro: Horkelia clevelandii, also known as Cleveland's horkelia, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Pacific coast of North America, belonging to the family Rosaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and gout. The bioactive constituents of Horkelia clevelandii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Horkelia clevelandii is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas and tinctures, often used to support joint health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Horkelia clevelandii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This classification places it within the order Rosales, a group of flowering plants that includes roses and other related species. Horkelia clevelandii is further categorized as a member of the genus Horkelia, a group of plants endemic to the western coast of North America. The species is native to the mountainous regions of California, where it grows in a variety of habitats. uses: Horkelia clevelandii is known to have been used by the indigenous people of North America for its medicinal properties.
